matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
seeded_markers: SIK3 p.Asn783Tyr (N783Y) (natural short sleep-associated missense variant)

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: An observed SIK3 variant places this sample in the curated SIK3 research context. The local SIK3 knowledge base focuses on emerging functional and sleep-trait biology. The locus has a growing GWAS and mechanistic literature, but interpretation is still mainly research grade for most variants. SIK3 is better characterized through pathway studies, sleep phenotypes, and selected rare functional variants than through a mature clinical common-SNP catalog.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
research_focus: Human and model-organism studies link SIK3 to sleep need, circadian or sleep-duration biology, and neuronal regulation.
Additional research connects SIK3 to metabolism, adiposity, and oncogenic or transcriptional signaling programs.
Sleep and circadian-biology studies.

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a SIK3 variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. SIK3 methylation is best treated as regulatory context around an emerging signaling gene. Interpret any promoter-proximal patterns as supportive evidence rather than as a direct proxy for sleep or metabolic phenotype. The local SIK3 knowledge base focuses on emerging functional and sleep-trait biology. The locus has a growing GWAS and mechanistic literature, but interpretation is still mainly research grade for most variants. SIK3 is better characterized through pathway studies, sleep phenotypes, and selected rare functional variants than through a mature clinical common-SNP catalog.

research_focus: Human and model-organism studies link SIK3 to sleep need, circadian or sleep-duration biology, and neuronal regulation.
